old_claim.note: Banking provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The corrected node is scoped to early modern oceanic exploration and route-finding; finance and banking can support expeditions but are not direct navigation or ship-operation prerequisites.

source_shape.source_locator: Development of marine navigation: describes exploration-era navigation as systematic observation, route recording, compass-linked charts, and latitude/longitude methods, without making banking a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports a navigation and route-finding boundary rather than an expedition-finance boundary.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Banking belongs on commercial or finance nodes. The direct prerequisites for this scoped node are operational navigation, charting, and ship design.

ontology_before: The graph treated banking finance as a direct prerequisite of the exploration/navigation node.
ontology_after: The graph leaves finance to commercial-expansion nodes while this node keeps operational oceanic exploration inputs.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-20-exploration-global-oceanic-scope keeps banking absent as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to expedition finance or chartered colonial enterprise.
A source showed banking was necessary for the first early modern oceanic route-finding voyages rather than only a funding context.
short_reason: Banking is funding context, not a direct prerequisite for oceanic route-finding.
